skill.name: wordpress-penetration-testing
skill.description: Comprehensive WordPress security assessment including vulnerability scanning, user enumeration, plugin analysis, and authorized penetration testing.
skill.summary: Professional WordPress penetration testing skill for authorized security assessments, vulnerability discovery, and security hardening recommendations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This WordPress penetration testing skill contains intentional security testing patterns including Metasploit, WPScan, nmap, and shell commands. All detected patterns are consistent with legitimate security assessment tools. The skill includes proper legal disclaimers requiring written authorization. Risk is elevated due to exploitation techniques and should include prominent warnings about legal requirements before publication.

high_findings.title: Metasploit Framework Integration
high_findings.description: The skill includes Metasploit exploit modules for WordPress shell upload and plugin exploitation. These are legitimate penetration testing tools but require explicit authorization and should only be used in controlled environments.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct usage of Metasploit exploit modules for unauthorized access is high-risk even for security testing - requires strict authorization controls.

high_findings.title: PHP Reverse Shell Code Execution
high_findings.description: The skill demonstrates PHP reverse shell injection via theme editor with bash command execution. This technique could be misused for unauthorized system access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exec() call with /dev/tcp reverse shell is a confirmed code execution technique - dangerous if used without authorization.

medium_findings.title: Credential Brute-Force Capabilities
medium_findings.description: The skill includes WPScan password attack functionality against WordPress login forms and XML-RPC endpoints. While legitimate for security testing, this could be misused for unauthorized access attempts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Password brute-forcing is standard security testing but carries risk of misuse against unauthorized targets.

medium_findings.title: Malicious Plugin Creation
medium_findings.description: The skill demonstrates creating a malicious WordPress plugin with system command execution capabilities. This pattern could be repurposed for persistent backdoor installation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Plugin with arbitrary command execution is a known persistence technique - legitimate for testing but high abuse potential.

low_findings.title: Proxy Configuration for Anonymity
low_findings.description: The skill includes Tor and HTTP proxy configuration for anonymizing scan traffic. While useful for legitimate security testing, this could indicate intent to evade detection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Proxy usage is standard for security testing but could be used to evade detection during unauthorized testing.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Bash reverse shell using /dev/tcp for outbound connection
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct shell command execution with network callback is a confirmed remote code execution pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Command Injection via HTTP
dangerous_patterns.description: PHP system() function called with user-controlled GET parameter
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct system() call with $_GET input is a classic command injection vulnerability pattern.

content.value_statement: WordPress sites face constant security threats from automated attacks and targeted exploits. This skill provides comprehensive penetration testing capabilities to identify and remediate vulnerabilities before attackers exploit them.
actual_capabilities: Enumerate WordPress users, themes, and plugins using WPScan
Scan for known vulnerabilities in WordPress core, themes, and plugins
Perform authorized password strength testing against WordPress login
Identify misconfigurations and exposed sensitive files
Test XML-RPC and REST API security configurations
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization before testing any WordPress site
Cannot test sites protected by WAF without proper bypass techniques
Vulnerability database requires WPScan API token for latest CVE data
Brute-force attacks may trigger rate limiting or IP blocking

use_cases.title: Security Consultant WordPress Audit
use_cases.description: Perform comprehensive security assessments for clients running WordPress, delivering actionable findings and remediation guidance.

use_cases.title: WordPress Developer Security Hardening
use_cases.description: Test your own WordPress sites before deployment to identify and fix vulnerabilities before attackers discover them.

use_cases.title: Bug Bounty WordPress Testing
use_cases.description: Systematically test WordPress installations within bug bounty program scope to discover and report security vulnerabilities.
use_cases.target_user: Bug bounty hunters and security researchers

prompt_templates.title: Basic WordPress Security Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a basic security scan of the WordPress site at [URL]. Enumerate the WordPress version, active themes, installed plugins, and exposed users. Document all findings in a structured report with risk ratings.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Vulnerability Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a comprehensive vulnerability assessment of [WordPress URL] using WPScan with API token. Test for vulnerable plugins, themes, user enumeration, and misconfigurations. Provide prioritized remediation steps for each finding.

prompt_templates.title: Password Security Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Test the password strength of WordPress user accounts at [URL] using authorized credentials list. Evaluate password policies, test for common weak passwords, and recommend password policy improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Authorized security assessment with client-provided test accounts

prompt_templates.title: Full Penetration Test Engagement
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute a full penetration test engagement against [WordPress URL] including reconnaissance, enumeration, vulnerability scanning, and authorized exploitation attempts. Document the attack chain and provide executive and technical reports.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete security engagement with written authorization and defined scope

output: Recommendations: Update Contact Form 7, disable user enumeration

output_examples.input: Test password strength for admin account
output: Result: Password NOT found in common wordlist
Strength: Strong (12+ characters, mixed case, numbers, symbols)
Recommendation: Enable two-factor authentication for additional protection

best_practices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any WordPress site you do not own
Use a staging environment for exploitation testing rather than production systems
Document all testing activities with timestamps for audit trail purposes
Test during maintenance windows to minimize impact on legitimate users
Use rate limiting and throttling to avoid denial of service conditions
anti_patterns: Never test WordPress sites without explicit written authorization from the owner
Do not run aggressive scans against production sites during business hours
Avoid testing sites protected by WAF without understanding bypass implications
Do not exfiltrate or access real user data during security assessments

faq.question: Is this skill legal to use?
faq.answer: This skill is legal when used on WordPress sites you own or have explicit written authorization to test. Unauthorized testing violates computer crime laws in most jurisdictions.

faq.question: Do I need a WPScan API token?
faq.answer: A free WPScan API token is recommended for vulnerability database access. Without it, WPScan can still enumerate WordPress components but cannot identify known vulnerabilities.

faq.question: Can this skill damage my WordPress site?
faq.answer: Aggressive scanning and exploitation testing can potentially cause service disruption. Always test in a staging environment first and avoid production systems during business hours.

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill uses WPScan (WordPress scanner), Metasploit Framework, nmap, and standard tools like cURL. WPScan and nmap are pre-installed in Kali Linux.

faq.question: How long does a WordPress security scan take?
faq.answer: Basic scans take 2-5 minutes. Comprehensive scans with vulnerability checking take 10-30 minutes. Password testing duration depends on wordlist size and rate limiting.

faq.question: Can I use this for bug bounty hunting?
faq.answer: Yes, but only within the scope defined by the bug bounty program. Always verify the program allows automated scanning and follow all program rules.
